task_description: Screen each abstract for relevance to: deep learning for radiology and medical imaging analysis. Label RELEVANT if the paper develops or evaluates a deep learning system for interpreting medical images (X-ray, CT, MRI, ultrasound, fundus, pathology slides). Label NOT_RELEVANT otherwise.

papers.title: U-Net with Attention Gates for Lung Nodule Segmentation in CT
papers.abstract: We extend U-Net with soft attention gates to focus on lung nodule regions during CT volume segmentation. Evaluated on the LIDC-IDRI dataset, our model achieves Dice 0.84 for nodule segmentation and sensitivity 0.91 at one false positive per scan.
papers.topic_hint: deep learning, CT segmentation, lung nodules
papers.methodology_hint: U-Net, attention gates, Dice, LIDC-IDRI benchmark
papers.claimed_contribution: Attention-gated U-Net for lung nodule segmentation achieving Dice 0.84 on LIDC-IDRI.

papers.title: Vision Transformer for Multi-Label Chest X-Ray Pathology Classification
papers.abstract: We adapt Vision Transformer (ViT-B/16) to classify 14 pathologies simultaneously from frontal chest X-rays. Fine-tuned on CheXpert, our model achieves mean AUC 0.90 across all pathologies and outperforms DenseNet-121 on 10 of 14 labels.
papers.topic_hint: vision transformer, chest X-ray, multi-label classification
papers.methodology_hint: ViT, CheXpert, mean AUC, DenseNet comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: ViT-based multi-label chest X-ray classifier with mean AUC 0.90 on CheXpert.

papers.title: Conditional GAN for MRI Data Augmentation in Brain Tumour Segmentation
papers.abstract: We train a conditional GAN to synthesise realistic brain MRI scans for data augmentation in tumour segmentation. Adding synthesised images to the training set of a 3D U-Net improves Dice on the BraTS 2021 validation set from 0.81 to 0.86.
papers.topic_hint: GAN, MRI augmentation, brain tumour segmentation
papers.claimed_contribution: cGAN augmentation strategy improving 3D U-Net tumour segmentation by 5 Dice points on BraTS 2021.

papers.title: EfficientNet for Diabetic Retinopathy Severity Grading
papers.abstract: We apply EfficientNet-B4 to grade diabetic retinopathy severity from colour fundus photographs at five levels. Trained on the EyePACS dataset with 88,000 images, our model achieves quadratic kappa 0.93 and outperforms prior inception-based graders.
papers.topic_hint: deep learning, diabetic retinopathy, fundus images
papers.methodology_hint: EfficientNet, EyePACS, quadratic kappa, severity grading
papers.claimed_contribution: EfficientNet-B4 grader for diabetic retinopathy achieving kappa 0.93 on EyePACS.

papers.title: Multi-Scale Attention Network for Fetal Biometry in Ultrasound
papers.abstract: We propose a multi-scale attention network that simultaneously localises and measures fetal biometric planes in third-trimester ultrasound. Evaluated on 3,000 scans from three clinical sites, our model agrees with sonographer measurements within 1.2mm mean error.
papers.methodology_hint: multi-scale attention, fetal measurement, sonographer agreement
papers.claimed_contribution: Attention network for fetal biometry in ultrasound with 1.2mm mean measurement error.

papers.title: Large Language Models for Legal Contract Summarisation
papers.abstract: We fine-tune GPT-4 on 50,000 commercial contracts to generate concise plain-language summaries of key obligations and risk clauses. Human evaluators rate our summaries as accurate and complete for 87% of contracts tested, outperforming zero-shot baselines by 23 percentage points.
papers.methodology_hint: GPT-4 fine-tuning, human evaluation, summarisation accuracy
papers.claimed_contribution: GPT-4-based contract summariser with 87% accuracy on key obligations and risk clauses.

papers.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for High-Frequency Trading Strategies
papers.abstract: We train a deep RL agent to execute high-frequency equity trading strategies using limit order book features. Backtested on two years of NASDAQ tick data, our agent achieves Sharpe ratio 2.4, outperforming both TWAP and benchmark momentum strategies.
papers.methodology_hint: deep RL, order book, Sharpe ratio, TWAP comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: Deep RL trading agent with Sharpe ratio 2.4, outperforming TWAP on NASDAQ tick data.

papers.title: Self-Supervised Learning for Autonomous Driving Perception
papers.abstract: We develop a contrastive learning framework for pretraining LiDAR point cloud encoders without manual annotation. Pretrained on 100,000 unlabelled driving scenes and fine-tuned with only 5% labelled data, our model achieves 93% of the performance of a fully supervised baseline.
papers.methodology_hint: contrastive learning, LiDAR pretraining, semi-supervised fine-tuning
papers.claimed_contribution: Contrastive LiDAR pretraining reaching 93% of supervised performance with 5% labelled data.

papers.title: Diffusion Models for High-Fidelity Text-to-Image Generation
papers.abstract: We present a latent diffusion model that synthesises photorealistic images from text prompts using a cross-attention mechanism trained on LAION-5B. Our model achieves FID 7.2 on MS-COCO and demonstrates superior prompt fidelity compared to DALL-E 2 and Imagen.
papers.claimed_contribution: Latent diffusion model with FID 7.2 on MS-COCO, outperforming DALL-E 2 and Imagen.

papers.title: Neural Architecture Search for Efficient Edge Inference
papers.abstract: We propose a differentiable neural architecture search method that optimises model accuracy and latency jointly for edge deployment on ARM Cortex-M devices. Our searched architectures achieve 92% ImageNet top-1 accuracy at 1ms inference latency on Cortex-M7.
papers.topic_hint: neural architecture search, edge inference, mobile deployment
papers.methodology_hint: differentiable NAS, latency optimisation, ImageNet, ARM Cortex-M
papers.claimed_contribution: Differentiable NAS producing edge models with 92% accuracy at 1ms latency on Cortex-M7.
